Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows mild hypoglycemia and low HbA1c, with elevated creatinine suggesting impaired renal function. Urinalysis reveals significant proteinuria, glycosuria, ketonuria, and abnormal urine appearance (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal), but no evidence of infection. Findings are consistent with possible diabetic nephropathy and renal impairment, despite low glucose/HbA1c. Recommend urgent clinical correlation for underlying metabolic or renal pathology and further evaluation.
